search_queries_used: site:academic.oup.com/bjd rosacea guideline 2021 periocular dermatitis eyelid
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic eye treatment or ophthalmology referral
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: The broad workflow can represent allergic, irritant, infectious, inflammatory, or ocular disease.
All treatment and referral decisions remain clinician-entered.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults or children with a periorbital or eyelid rash; eye pain, visual symptoms, infection, contact exposure, childhood, and pregnancy need separate assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE primary, dermatology, allergy, and ophthalmology assessment.
UAE_applicability: Selected morphology is transferable, but UAE eye red-flag, infection, medicine, emergency, and referral pathways remain required.
recency_verification: The primary BAD 2021 guideline full text was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: No newer BAD rosacea guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact periocular-dermatitis discussion supports documenting eyelid or periorbital papules, pustules, redness, scale, itch, burning or soreness and corticosteroid-eye-ointment exposure only when assessed; it does not cover all causes of a periorbital rash.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting abrupt or persistent perioral or periocular papules and pustules, redness, scale, lip-margin sparing, itch, burning, soreness, eyelid involvement, and topical, inhaled, or ophthalmic corticosteroid exposure without inferring cause.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Ocular and periocular symptom context was reviewed, but no ocular diagnosis, medication, or referral is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician documentation of facial distribution, erythema, papules or pustules, flushing, burning or stinging, dryness, edema, and ocular symptoms without inferring diagnosis.

unresolved_source_gaps: Vision, eye pain, photophobia, discharge, swelling, laterality, precise morphology, contact and medicine exposure, diagnosis, investigations, treatment, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
